item.title: How Switzerland Is Adpating to Melting Glaciers
item.description: Climate change is leading to the melting of glaciers and the thawing of permafrost worldwide.

item.title: Peugeot Revival Takes Hold on EV Demand Surge in France, Germany
item.description: Stellantis NV’s Peugeot brand is getting a boost from rising electric-vehicle demand, helping the nameplate emerge from quality issues that tarnished its image and drove away customers.

item.title: California Overhauls Carbon Market Over Affordability Concerns
item.description: State makes $4 billion concession to oil industry amid soaring gasoline prices.

item.title: SEC Moves to Scrap Biden-Era Rule on Climate Risk Disclosures
item.description: The US Securities and Exchange Commission wants to formally kill rules it’s now calling a “dramatic overreach” of its own authority that would have required public companies to report greenhouse gas emissions and climate-related risks to their operations.

item.title: Burberry Pushes Off Net Zero Goal by 10 Years
item.description: The company called the step a pragmatic response to market conditions.

item.title: South Africa Eyes Green Bonds For $228 Billion ESG Finance Plan
item.description: South Africa’s Treasury published a framework for sustainable-finance instruments to help raise the 3.7 trillion rand ($228 billion) needed to mitigate the effect of greenhouse—gas emissions over the next decade.

item.title: Paris Heat Wave Set to Peak Before Weekend Relief
item.description: The record-breaking heat wave scorching Europe is forecast to peak Friday in Paris, but relief is on the horizon.

item.title: Indian Power Firms Snap Up Gas to Meet Night-Time Cooling Demand
item.description: India’s power generation companies have quadrupled their natural gas purchases from a domestic bourse over the past couple of months to meet surging power demand from searing summer heat.

item.title: Europe Faces Biggest Food Price Shock in G7 From Hotter World
item.description: A global food crunch due to extreme weather would boost prices more in the euro area than in Group of Seven economies, according to Oxford Economics.

item.title: India Cuts Monsoon Forecast to 90% of Normal in Risk to Crops
item.description: India lowered its forecast for this year’s monsoon, a blow to farmers who are already contending with higher input costs due to the Middle East conflict.

item.title: Rise of Renewables and EVs in Australia Is Trimming Emissions
item.description: Additions of renewable energy, lower use of fossil fuels and accelerating adoption of electric vehicles cut Australia’s annual greenhouse gas emissions by about 2%, helping to ease concerns the nation — one of the world’s top per-capita polluters — will miss climate targets.

item.title: Americans Doubt Countries Will Do Enough to Tame Climate Change
item.description: Pew Research Center polling finds a majority of US adults view global warming as a significant problem, though with a sharp partisan divide.

item.title: Reform UK Pushes North Sea in Biggest-Yet Energy Firm Meeting
item.description: Reform UK Deputy Leader Richard Tice told energy giants his poll-leading party would focus on cutting bills rather than investing in green energy if it wins the next general election.

item.title: Bezos Earth Fund Lags Behind $10 Billion Spending Goal
item.description: The philanthropy says it’s committed to fulfilling the pledge by 2030.

item.title: Burberry Delays Net Zero Emissions Target by 10 Years to 2050
item.description: Burberry Group Plc postponed its goal of cutting emissions to net zero by a decade to 2050, in a setback for the British luxury brand’s bid to become an industry leader in sustainability.

item.title: Bezos Earth Fund Is Off Pace to Meet $10 Billion Climate Giving Pledge
item.description: The philanthropy says it’s still committed to donating a record amount to environmental causes by the end of the decade. Most of the promised funds remain unspent.

item.title: Global Warming Likely to Exceed 1.5C Through 2030, WMO Says
item.description: One year in the next four will likely be the hottest ever recorded, the agency said.

item.title: Germans Switch to EVs at Record Pace Thanks to Government Bonus
item.description: More German drivers switched to an electric vehicle in the first quarter of this year than ever before after the government launched a major subsidy program that can shave as much as €6,000 ($6,967) off the price of a new car.

item.title: HSBC, Barclays Among Banks Set to Lose Cambridge University Cash
item.description: Barclays Plc and HSBC Holdings Plc are among UK banks set to lose cash holdings from Cambridge University as it starts pulling hundreds of millions of pounds away from institutions that finance fossil fuels.

item.title: Stonepeak, Lim Family Said to Weigh Withdrawing Yinson Bid
item.description: Yinson Holdings Bhd.’s founding Lim family and Stonepeak Partners are considering withdrawing a plan to take the energy infrastructure company private, according to people familiar with the information.

item.title: London’s Expats Struggle to Adapt to Extreme Heat Without Air Conditioning
item.description: Vanessa Chan, who moved to London from Hong Kong three years ago, has been stunned by the extreme heat she’s experienced in the UK capital over the past few days.

item.title: China’s Crop Belt Faces Flood Risk as Heavy Rains Arrive Early
item.description: Heavy rains are arriving earlier and lasting longer across several parts of China this year, raising risks for agriculture and disaster management.

item.title: These Countries Are Plotting the End of the Fossil Fuel Era
item.description: Tzeporah Berman, chair of the Fossil Fuel Non-Proliferation Treaty Initiative, hopes to bring the most ambitious nations together to end the use of oil, gas and coal.

item.title: The Swiss Alps Reckon With the Threat of Collapsing Glaciers
item.description: A year after the town of Blatten was destroyed, Switzerland faces difficult decisions over the risks and costs of living in the mountains.

item.title: Data Center Firm Backed by Oaktree Plans to Sell Carbon Credits to Hyperscalers
item.description: Pure Data Centres, a UK firm backed by private credit specialist Oaktree Capital Management, is launching a platform to sell carbon-removal credits to large cloud-computing providers.

item.title: El Niño Threat Prompts Peru to Extend Halt on Anchovy Fishing
item.description: Peru has extended a halt on anchovy fishing due to a warming of the Pacific waters off the country’s coast, as the threat of a weather-roiling El Niño looms.

item.title: Oil Barges Forced to Carry Less Fuel as Rhine Water Level Drops
item.description: Falling water levels on one of Europe’s most important rivers are restricting the amount of oil barges can carry, adding more pressure to the region’s fuel supply chains as the Iran war grinds on.

item.title: Iran War Pollution Spread Across Thousands of Miles, Research
item.description: Fires at oil facilities in March produced a plume the size of Italy.

item.title: Ex-Environment Minister to Quit Carney’s Caucus After Climate Reversals
item.description: A former Canadian environment minister will resign his seat in Prime Minister Mark Carney’s caucus after the government watered down its climate policy.

item.title: Why Building the Best Grid Is a Key to the Future
item.description: As EVs and AI spur a rapid rise in electricity demand, innovators are developing solutions that could bring clean, stable power to people around the world.
